item.title: 175 more Bangladeshis return from Libya
item.description: Another 175 Bangladeshi nationals returned home from Libya on Thursday....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Parents of rape accused sent to jail as son skips court
item.description: The Rajshahi Child Rape Prevention Tribunal on Thursday sent the parents of the a rape accused to jail after cancelling their bail, as their son failed to appear before the court on the scheduled date of the case....

item.title: HADI MURDER: Submission of further probe report deferred for 7th time
item.description: The time for submitting a further investigation report, in the case of the killing of Inquilab Mancha convener Sharif Osman Hadi, has been extended by 13 more days....

item.title: 4 arrested for abducting secretariat official’s son
item.description: The police on Wednesday night arrested four youths over the abduction of a secretariat official’s son on Tuesday....

item.title: BRRI holds training for scientists, officers
item.description: The Bangladesh Rice Research Institute held an induction training for the newly recruited scientists and officers of grade 9 and above at the conference room of the Training Division of BRRI in Gazipur on Thursday....

item.title: 10 hurt, houses, cars damaged in Cox’s Bazar LPG station fire
item.description: At least 10 people were injured in a fire triggered by an explosion at an unauthorised LPG filling station in the Kolatoli area of Cox’s Bazar on Wednesday night....

item.title: AIUB student found dead at Bashundhara
item.description: The police on Thursday recovered the hanging body of a private university student from her rented flat at Bashundhara residential area in the capital Dhaka....

item.title: AL offices keep reopening
item.description: Leaders and activists of the Bangladesh Awami League, activities of which are banned, have continued reopening offices of the party in cities, districts, upazilas and unions since the Bangladesh Nationalist Party came to power, with such an incident......

item.title: Policing as spectacle, power as signal
item.description: IN RECENT weeks, one police officer has once again come to symbolise a deeper truth about policing in Bangladesh. Mohammad Masud Alam, deputy commissioner of Ramna Division, embodies a style of policing that is performative and deeply invested in visibility. Videos circulating online show him leading anti-narcotics drives in public parks, confronting young......

item.title: Reality and ideal in politics and education
item.description: WHAT began on February 22 as a simple inquiry about two new young entrants in our political circle gradually unfolded into a probing conversation with AI about politics, education and moral responsibility in Bangladesh. I had not anticipated anything profound. I was merely curious. Yet curiosity, when followed patiently, sometimes opens doors that one did not intend to enter......

item.title: Surprising appointment and disquieting dismissal
item.description: THE appointment of a businessman as governor of the Bangladesh Bank marks more than a routine change of leadership. It signals a potential shift in the monetary and regulatory philosophy. For economists and policy observers, the move raises profound concern about institutional independence, policy credibility and the trajectory of financial sector reforms......

item.title: MRT cost overrun proposals demand early govt review
item.description: A MODERN metro system inb Dhaka can offer immense public benefit, but the inflating cost trajectory, as per the contractors’ proposal, of the forthcoming lines raises serious concern. The reported escalation of MRT Line 1 cost from about Tk 52,500 crore to about Tk 96,500 crore and of the northern route of MRT Line 5 cost from Tk 41,200 crore to about Tk 88,000 crore can......

item.title: Indecency and impropriety in central bank shake-up
item.description: THIS is customary for a new government to replace people in important positions of state governance. The governor of the Bangladesh Bank, the regulator of the monetary and financial system, is one such position. And the Bangladesh Nationalist Party government, formed with a landslide victory in the 13th national elections held on February 12, on February 25 appointed......

item.title: US govt accused of major ‘cover-up’ over Trump sex abuse claims
item.description: Democrats on Wednesday accused US president Donald Trump’s administration of the ‘largest government cover-up in modern history’ over reports that it withheld documents relating to allegations that the Republican leader sexually abused a minor....

item.title: Iran, US hold talks in push to avert war
item.description: Iran and the United States held talks in Switzerland on Thursday, in a last-ditch bid to avert war under the shadow of the biggest American military build-up in the Middle East in decades....

item.title: 8,000 died or vanished on migrant routes in 2025: UN
item.description: At least 7,667 people died or went missing last year on migration routes around the world, but the true death toll is likely higher, the UN’s migration agency reported Thursday....

item.title: 20 killed in Pakistan attacks
item.description: Nearly 20 police personnel and civilians have been killed in a wave of militant attacks across northwest Pakistan over the past two days, a police official said on Thursday, in the latest surge of violence to hit the restive border region....

item.title: Youth arrested for Hazaribagh schoolgirl murder
item.description: The police on Thursday arrested a youth over stabbing a school girl to death in the Charakghata area of Hazaribagh in Dhaka on Wednesday night. ...Maps

item.title: US, Ukraine hold talks in Geneva
item.description: US and Ukrainian officials were meeting in Geneva on Thursday to discuss advancing efforts to end Russia’s four-year invasion of Ukraine, just as Moscow signalled it was in no hurry to sign a deal....

item.title: Urs at Mia Saheb’s Maidan khanquah Friday
item.description: The annual urs marking the 289th death anniversary of Hazrat Maulana Shah Sufi Sayed Abdur Rahim Shaheed (RA), founder of the Mia Saheber Maidan Khanquah Sarif, and 10th descendant of Boro Pir Hazrat Abdul Kader Jilani (RA) will be held on the......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Half-decomposed body recovered in Gazipur forest
item.description: The police recovered the half-decomposed body of a youth at the Banshari Resort of Shalban in Gazipur on Thursday....

item.title: Vishnu’s touchstone statue recovered in Naogaon
item.description: Police recovered a touchstone idol of the Hindu deity Vishnu weighing approximately 137 kilograms from Porsha upazila of Naogaon....

item.title: 14 arrested over two BNP factions clash in Magura
item.description: The police have arrested 14 leaders and activists of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party in connection with a clash between two BNP groups over distribution of government rice cards in Magura....

item.title: Sand-carrying vessels suspended for 10 days for Eid travel
item.description: The government has announced a nationwide suspension of sand-carrying bulkhead and dinghy boat movements from March 16 to 25 to ensure safe travel for passengers heading home during the forthcoming holy Eid-ul-Fitr....Daily news updates

item.title: Displaying govt-set fees for land services directed
item.description: The ministry of land has issued directives to all deputy commissioners to display the government-fixed fees for land services through permanent signboards....

item.title: Probe finds irregularities in lift supply to RMCH
item.description: A probe committee formed by Rajshahi Medical College Hospital has found serious irregularities in the supply of a lift supplied for its intensive care unit, raising allegations of fraud over the equipment’s origin and documentation....

item.title: UNDP boosts EC capacity with IT assistance
item.description: The United Nations Development Programme on Thursday handed over 30 desktop computers to the Electoral Training Institute, reinforcing institutional capacity for electoral training and knowledge management in Bangladesh....

item.title: Dhaka sees Myanmar FM, AA chief’s messages positively
item.description: Amid Bangladesh’s continued engagement, Myanmar’s union minister for foreign affairs Than Swe and chairman of the United League of Arakan Twan Mrat Naing have congratulated recently appointed Bangladesh foreign minister Khalilur Rahman which is seen positively byDhaka....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Motorcyclist crushed under train in Kushtia
item.description: A motorcyclist was killed and another injured critically after being crushed under a train in Purba Majampur area in Kushtia Sadar upazila Thursday morning....

item.title: BNP picks Rezaul for Bogura-6 by-election
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party has nominated Rezaul Karim Badsha as its candidate for the by-election to Bogura-6, the seat vacated by BNP chairman Tarique Rahman, now prime minister, party sources said....

item.title: LPG REVIEW MEETING: Monitoring by local admin to increase
item.description: Commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir on Thursday said that monitoring by the local administration would be increased to check the price manipulation of the liquefied petroleum gas in the market....

item.title: Nahid demands new BB governor’s removal
item.description: Md Nahid Islam, convener of the National Citizen Party, on Thursday demanded the removal of the newly appointed governor of Bangladesh Bank....

item.title: Govt reconstitutes upazila law-order committees
item.description: The home ministry on Thursday reconstituted the upazila law and order committee across the country with expanded mandate of adopting steps required maintain stability within their purviews alongside assessing crime trends and reviewing law and order situation....

item.title: School boy among 5 killed in road accidents
item.description: At least five people, including a school boy, were killed in road accidents in Gazipur, Satkhira, Kishoreganj and cumilla districts on Thursday....

item.title: 100 held in Mohammadpur, Adabor drives
item.description: Police have detained 100 people in a special drive aimed at curbing crimes of teen gangs, extortion and mugging in Dhaka’s Mohammadpur and Adabor areas....

item.title: Jamaat, BNP candidates in 4 seats seek ballot recount in HC
item.description: The defeated candidates of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami and the Bangladesh Nationalist Party for 4 parliamentary seats in the 13th Jatiya Sangsad elections have filed separate petitions with the High Court alleging irregularities in the polls and demanding a ballot recount....

item.title: Jamaat concerned over insecurity of women
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Thursday expressed concern over insecurity of the girl students and women in the country....

item.title: ACC arrests PIO with bribe money in Feni
item.description: A project implementation officer of Sonagazi upazila in Feni was caught red-handed with bribe money during a trap operation conducted by the Anti-Corruption Commission on Thursday....

item.title: Police SI suspended in Sylhet
item.description: Sylhet district police suspended a sub-inspector of Companiganj police station on Wednesday after his walkie-talkie was recovered from two youths in Sylhet city early Tuesday....

item.title: Road minister remains adamant on extortion remarks
item.description: The road transport and bridges, railways and shipping minister, Sheikh Rabiul Alam, on Thursday again said that he did not consider the money collected in the name of different road transport organisations on mutual understanding as extortion....
